## Acceptance Criteria

Fail. `jit graph rdeps` now defaults to depth 1 at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:1477`, but the top-level `jit rdeps` alias remains `default_value = "0"` at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:160`. That alias is explicitly documented as a top-level alias for `jit graph rdeps` at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:152`, and `main.rs` normalizes it into `GraphCommands::Rdeps` while preserving the parsed depth at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1017`. Result: unbounded reverse-dependency traversal is still available by default through `jit rdeps <id>`, so unbounded traversal is not fully opt-in.

## Architecture And Separation Of Concerns

The changed canonical graph CLI path stays in `cli.rs`, and traversal execution remains in `commands/graph.rs`. I did not find a new domain/storage/CLI boundary violation in the reviewed change.

## Functional Paradigm

No blocking functional-style issue. `show_rdeps_with_depth` uses imperative BFS state in `crates/jit/src/commands/graph.rs:165`, which is a reasonable graph traversal exception.

## Correctness And Invariants

Blocking correctness issue: the reverse-dependency alias still defaults to unbounded traversal. `show_rdeps_with_depth` treats depth 0 as unlimited at `crates/jit/src/commands/graph.rs:153`, so the alias default materially changes behavior.

No new file writes, event logging paths, gate transition paths, assignee/label validation, PID handling, or unsafe code were introduced by this change.

## Safety

Pass. `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` is present in `crates/jit/src/lib.rs:6` and `crates/jit/src/main.rs:14`; no unsafe code was found in the touched source paths.

## Testing

Provided issue evidence shows `cargo-ci` passed with exit code 0 at `2026-06-28T00:58:10.512400100+00:00`; I did not rerun tests.

The new tests cover canonical `jit graph rdeps` default and `--depth 0` behavior in `crates/jit/tests/graph_depth_tests.rs:302` and `crates/jit/tests/graph_depth_tests.rs:338`, but they do not cover the top-level `jit rdeps` alias. The existing alias test only uses a one-hop graph at `crates/jit/tests/first_guess_residuals_test.rs:169`, so unbounded and bounded results are identical and the regression is missed.

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.

## Error Handling

No new fallible production operation or error type was introduced in the reviewed change. No new production `unwrap()` or `expect()` issue was found in the touched source; new unwraps are in tests.

## CLI Contract

Fail for command consistency. `jit rdeps` is exposed as the top-level alias for `jit graph rdeps`, but its default depth remains different from the canonical command.

## Documentation

Canonical `graph rdeps` doc-comments were updated at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:1458`. The top-level alias documentation at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:152` was not updated to document bounded default or opt-in unbounded traversal, and the implementation still contradicts the canonical behavior.

## Prior Review Feedback

No prior review history was provided for this issue.

## JIT Issue Management

Dependency `527bf0a6` is marked done in the provided issue context. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.

## Findings

1. `jit rdeps <id>` still defaults to unbounded traversal because the top-level alias keeps `#[arg(long, default_value = "0")]` at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:160`, is normalized into `GraphCommands::Rdeps` at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1017`, and depth 0 means unlimited in `crates/jit/src/commands/graph.rs:153`. This violates REQ-19’s requirement that reverse-dependency traversal be bounded by default and unbounded only by opt-in, consistent with `graph deps`.

2. Test coverage misses the alias regression: new tests cover only `jit graph rdeps`, while the existing alias equivalence test at `crates/jit/tests/first_guess_residuals_test.rs:169` uses only a one-hop graph, where bounded and unbounded outputs match. Add a multi-level `jit rdeps` test asserting the default excludes transitive dependents and `--depth 0` includes them.

Total findings: 2

VERDICT: FAIL
